pi_items.source: Confirmed via find_foley_route aliases logic in verifier.py
Confirmed via calculation (2.0s * 44100Hz = 88200) matching instructions in spotting_notes.txt and verifier.py
Confirmed via calculation from the exact spotting note cues (5.0s, 10.0s, 15.0s, 20.0s) multiplied by 44100Hz sample rate
Confirmed via tolerance threshold check in verifier.py (representing 0.5s at 44100Hz)
Confirmed via pass_threshold condition in verifier.py

privileged_info_summary: The task utilizes real public domain audio recordings (such as Beethoven's Moonlight Sonata downloaded from the Internet Archive) as source material. These files are dynamically cropped to 30 seconds and renamed to 'raw_footsteps.wav' by the setup script to simulate a Foley recording. I verified all metadata claims by analyzing setup_task.sh and verifier.py. The cues_samples accurately reflect the spotting notes (5.0s, 10.0s, 15.0s, and 20.0s) converted to 44100 Hz (220500, 441000, 661500, 882000), and max_region_length_samples correctly matches the 2.0-second length constraint (88200 samples). Track aliases, tolerance, and passing thresholds precisely match the verifier logic.
